_comment: Migrations the prod DB ledger (workers_schema_version) reports as NOT recorded, that are KNOWN and accepted as of the audit date — so the predeploy migration gate does not cry-wolf on them. The gate blocks only on missing migrations NOT in this list (genuine new deploy-ahead-of-schema drift), plus the placeholder/unreachable DATABASE_URL footguns. Burn this list down: when a 'staged_absent' entry is applied to prod, remove it; when an 'applied_unrecorded' entry's version is back-filled into the ledger, remove it.
audit_method: compared src/workers/db/migrations vs prod workers_schema_version + to_regclass() table-existence probes on prod flat-truth-23350426

accepted_pending.evidence: customer_api_tokens table EXISTS on prod; only the ledger row is missing
accepted_pending.action: back-fill ledger: INSERT INTO workers_schema_version (version,...) VALUES (37,...)

accepted_pending.evidence: documents table EXISTS on prod; only the ledger row is missing
ALTER-only migration (admits outlook OAuth provider); table/column existence not yet confirmed
accepted_pending.action: verify outlook columns/constraints exist on prod; back-fill ledger or apply

accepted_pending.evidence: mcp_access_log table ABSENT; header says 'STAGED until operator applies'
accepted_pending.action: operator cutover: apply when MCP access-logging is activated

accepted_pending.evidence: llm_usage_log table ABSENT; header says 'STAGED until operator applies'
accepted_pending.action: operator cutover: apply when per-tenant LLM metering is activated

accepted_pending.evidence: idempotency_keys table ABSENT; header says 'STAGED until operator applies'
accepted_pending.action: operator cutover: apply on the write-path cutover
